title: Authenticated RCE via Custom MCP stdio server config (MCP/core.ts)
description: Flowise lets an authenticated user register a Custom MCP stdio server, supplying a command and args that are spawned via StdioClientTransport. validateMCPServerConfig() in packages/components/nodes/tools/MCP/core.ts restricts command to an allowlist (node, npx, python, python3, docker) and runs validateArgsForLocalFileAccess() and validateCommandInjection() over the args. However those arg checks only reject shell metacharacters and local-file paths; they do not reject code-execution flags. An attacker can therefore submit an allowlisted command with a dangerous flag, e.g. {"command":"npx","args":["-c","touch /tmp/pwn"]} (or node -e / python -c), which passes all checks and results in arbitrary command execution on the host when the MCP transport launches the process.
